text:
Once upon a time in a little town nestled by towering mountainsides, there was a tiny owl named Oliver. Oliver loved to listen to the tales whispered by wind Through branches tall.

One evening, Oliver nestled into his favourite oak hollow underneath an old great elm. He listened intently as the old oak owl recounted a story about a wise old owl who taught an owl-eyed little orphan bird about friendship.

Legend had it that an old owl long gone loved nothing more than instructing young owlets. One day, a orphan tiny and scared flew into his nesting site. Old Owl didn Owl didn't discriminate. Old Owl welcomed the little orphan owl.

Together they listened to stories from old maps that talked about hidden treasures deep in the mountains. They shared dreams about snowflakes drifting gracefully. Old Owl Owl showed him how to fly gracefully. That winter season, they lived happily ever after.

Sometimes when Oliver listened closely at night, he could hear Old Owlowlowlowl owl-ing tales to the stars.
